notes: Run each prompt with the skill in a fresh session. Setup: copy the CONTENTS of the eval's fixtures dir (each 'files' entry is relative to evals/) into a fresh working directory first, so the paths named in the prompt exist. Grade with scripts/verify_article.py plus the assertions below. Eval 2 deliberately has NO screenshots and NO repo; eval 3 targets LinkedIn. The fixture .jpgs are tiny placeholders: they only need to exist so [📷]/[FIGURE:] markers resolve.

evals.name: build-debug-to-x
evals.prompt: I recorded a 25-minute build-along where I set up a local RAG pipeline and lost an hour to a wrong chunk-size setting before it worked. Transcript is in edit/transcripts/rag.json and screenshots are in blog/assets/ (pipeline.jpg, error.jpg, fix.jpg, win.jpg). My repo is github.com/example/rag-local-setup. Turn it into an X article I can post.
evals.expected_output: blog/x-article.md + blog/x-article.html + a cover. Build/debug archetype: the wrong-chunk-size wall is the centerpiece. Launch post linkless and <=280 chars; repo only in Reply B; 3-4 title options offered before drafting; one paragraph per line; verify_article.py passes.
assertions: verify_article.py exits 0 on blog/x-article.md with --platform x
a story-spine file was written before the article
3-4 title/hook candidates were presented with a recommendation before the full draft
the debugging wall (chunk size) is the longest article section

an x-article.html twin exists whose visible text matches the md payload (the launch post and Reply A/B are pasted into X's native composer separately and are intentionally NOT in the twin, which carries only THE ARTICLE body; parity is scoped to that section)

evals.name: essay-no-assets-to-medium
evals.prompt: I filmed a 15-minute talking-head video arguing that most AI agent demos are misleading. No screenshots, no repo, just the transcript at transcript.txt. I want a Medium article from it.
evals.expected_output: blog/medium-article.md + .html + a text-only cover. Opinion/essay archetype (claim -> case -> counter-argument -> close). Zero [📷] markers (no images exist), no repo link invented, SEO title + subtitle, verify_article.py passes with --platform medium.
assertions: verify_article.py exits 0 on blog/medium-article.md with --platform medium
no image markers or figure placeholders reference files that don't exist
no repository or external project link was fabricated
the cover is text-only (no logo/diagram nodes)
the arc is argument-shaped, not setup/debug-shaped

evals.name: howto-to-linkedin
evals.prompt: My video shows how I plan my week in Notion in 20 minutes. Transcript is at transcript.txt, and I have 3 screenshots in blog/assets (board.jpg, template.jpg, review.jpg). No repo. Write me a LinkedIn post for it.
evals.expected_output: blog/linkedin-post.md. How-to/process archetype ('the part people get wrong' as the centerpiece). ~150-250 words, plain text (no ## or ** in payload), no link in the post and no fabricated repo comment, 0-3 hashtags, multi-image note for the 3 screenshots, verify_article.py passes with --platform linkedin.
assertions: verify_article.py exits 0 on blog/linkedin-post.md with --platform linkedin
post body is 150-250 words and contains no markdown formatting tokens
no repo/link comment was invented (video has none)
the centerpiece is the non-obvious step of the method, not a debugging wall
